party_actions.rule: When a brown lesion shows dark brown-to-black irregular clumps concentrated asymmetrically in one portion, with at least three distinct shades of brown from periphery to center, and an irregular fading border, classify as Melanoma.
preconditions: Dark brown or near-black irregular clumps or blotches are clustered asymmetrically in one region of the lesion rather than distributed evenly
At least three distinct color tones are visible within the lesion: light tan at the edges, medium brown in the body, and dark brown/black concentrated off-center
The lesion border is irregular with pigment fading unevenly — some edges are sharp while others trail off diffusely into surrounding skin
party_actions.rationale: Benign nevi typically show symmetric pigment distribution with one or two uniform shades of brown and smooth borders. The combination of asymmetrically clustered dark clumps, three or more color tones, and an irregular fading border indicates disordered melanocytic proliferation characteristic of melanoma. The PUPIL likely saw the small size and brown color and defaulted to nevus without assessing the internal structural asymmetry and color heterogeneity.

party_actions.validator_observations: The lesion does show dark brown/near-black clumps that appear somewhat asymmetrically concentrated toward the center-upper portion, partially satisfying the first condition. However, the color variation is limited — predominantly medium-to-dark brown with some darker central speckling, but not clearly three distinct tones (light tan at edges, medium brown in body, dark brown/black off-center). The border is somewhat irregular but the pigment fading is relatively uniform around the periphery rather than showing sharply defined edges on some sides and diffuse trailing on others. Not all three pre-conditions are clearly met.

rounds.validator_observations: The lesion does show dark brown/near-black clumps that appear somewhat asymmetrically concentrated toward the center-upper portion, partially satisfying the first condition. However, the color variation is limited — predominantly medium-to-dark brown with some darker central speckling, but not clearly three distinct tones (light tan at edges, medium brown in body, dark brown/black off-center). The border is somewhat irregular but the pigment fading is relatively uniform around the periphery rather than showing sharply defined edges on some sides and diffuse trailing on others. Not all three pre-conditions are clearly met.

party_actions.rule: When a brown lesion shows dark brown/near-black clumps somewhat asymmetrically concentrated toward one portion, with darker central speckling against a medium-to-dark brown background, and a somewhat irregular border, classify as Melanoma.
preconditions: Dark brown or near-black clumps are somewhat asymmetrically concentrated toward the center-upper portion of the lesion
Darker speckling is visible centrally against a predominantly medium-to-dark brown background
The lesion border is somewhat irregular with pigment fading at the periphery
party_actions.rationale: Asymmetrically distributed dark clumps with central speckling within a brown lesion indicate disordered melanocyte proliferation at varying depths, a hallmark of melanoma. The combination of structural asymmetry (off-center dark aggregates) and color heterogeneity (speckling over a brown base) distinguishes melanoma from benign nevi, which tend to show uniform pigment distribution and homogeneous color patterns.

party_actions.validator_observations: The lesion shows dark brown/near-black clumps asymmetrically concentrated toward the upper-central portion. There is darker central speckling against a medium-to-dark brown background. The border is somewhat irregular with pigment fading at the periphery, particularly at the lower and lateral edges. All three pre-conditions appear to be met.

rounds.validator_observations: The lesion shows dark brown/near-black clumps asymmetrically concentrated toward the upper-central portion. There is darker central speckling against a medium-to-dark brown background. The border is somewhat irregular with pigment fading at the periphery, particularly at the lower and lateral edges. All three pre-conditions appear to be met.

final_rule.rule: When a brown lesion shows dark brown/near-black clumps somewhat asymmetrically concentrated toward one portion, with darker central speckling against a medium-to-dark brown background, and a somewhat irregular border, classify as Melanoma.
preconditions: Dark brown or near-black clumps are somewhat asymmetrically concentrated toward the center-upper portion of the lesion
Darker speckling is visible centrally against a predominantly medium-to-dark brown background
The lesion border is somewhat irregular with pigment fading at the periphery
final_rule.rationale: Asymmetrically distributed dark clumps with central speckling within a brown lesion indicate disordered melanocyte proliferation at varying depths, a hallmark of melanoma. The combination of structural asymmetry (off-center dark aggregates) and color heterogeneity (speckling over a brown base) distinguishes melanoma from benign nevi, which tend to show uniform pigment distribution and homogeneous color patterns.

tighten_history.precondition: The lesion border shows at least one segment where pigment extends outward in an irregular tongue-like or jagged protrusion rather than fading smoothly and gradually into the surrounding skin
tighten_history.validator_observations: The lesion shows a small brown lesion with some darker central clumping and speckling, which partially satisfies the first two pre-conditions. However, the border does not clearly show irregular tongue-like or jagged protrusions extending outward - it appears relatively rounded and fades fairly smoothly at the periphery. The fourth pre-condition (irregular tongue-like or jagged protrusion at the border) is not clearly met. Additionally, while there is some asymmetry, the overall lesion is small and the border irregularity required by the rule is not convincingly present.

tighten_history.precondition: The lesion contains at least two or three distinct brown color zones (e.g., light tan, medium brown, and dark brown) arranged as separate patches or islands within the lesion rather than forming a single smooth gradient from center to edge

The lesion contains at least two visibly distinct shades of brown (e.g., light tan, medium brown, and dark brown/black) arranged in discrete, non-concentric patches rather than a smooth gradient from center to edge
